name: Q664655
abstract: Wendell Philips Culley (January 8, 1906 in Worcester, Massachusetts – May 8, 1983 in Los Angeles, CA) was an American jazz trumpeter and occasional multi instrumentalist. Growing up in Worcester, Culley played regularly at the AME Zion Church, in the Commerce High School band, and occasionally with Mamie Moffitt and Her Five Jazz Hounds.

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: Thad, Joe Newman, Wendell Culley and me. That was a good trumpet section... we all liked one another real well.
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: Wendell Culley was there. He was playing lead... So that’s one of the reasons I was hired.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The trumpet section had Wendell Culley, Clarence Wheeler, George Wingfield and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well, now, Wendell Culley was up there, too, wasn't he? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, he was from Providence, too... No, he was from Worcester -- Worcester, Massachusetts.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: One of the students that I’ve heard actually do that is Judith. Judith sang – she sings the solo of Wendell Culley on Little Darlin’.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Count Basie... There's Wendell Culley ended up all his life playing with Count Basie
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
